satire_attempt: Today on Senior Engineer Yoga: discover the ancient Vedic secret that you should both think AND ship. Mind-blowing. Subscribe to my course Architectural Reading & Shipping Discipline: They Are Two Disciplines, Not One ($1,997 launch price, 50% off if you commit to a quarter of practice).
verdict_reason: The satire CAN be written but the engineering layer specifically guards against the platitude it mocks. The verse's META structure (Krishna naming the architecture of his teaching) is preserved as the engineering staff/principal naming the structure of the team's work — which is a real organizational pathology that real teams collapse. The engineering layer specifies the operational forums (architecture review channel; GitHub merge cadence; Sentry error budgets) and the consequence of collapse (work stops reducing the underlying problem). The satire bounces off because the actual content is operationally specific, not a slogan.
